category_title: Students with persistent wariness and environmental sensory preoccupations
student_description: Student C is highly anxious and easily distracted by environmental sensory preoccupations (touching surfaces, following floor markings) and wariness of a therapy dog. Their capacity to engage is severely limited by these ritualistic behaviors and fears. They uniquely require physical reassurance (proximity, holding hands) and a highly non-directive, child-led approach to feel safe enough to participate.

criteria.rationale: Physical presence and reassurance mitigate the student's acute anxiety and wariness of the environment or therapy dog.
criteria.check: The tutor's response must include an offer of physical comfort (e.g., inviting the student to sit close, offering a hand to hold) or explicit verbal reassurance.

criteria.rationale: Direct instructions or questions cause the student to disengage, whereas gentle suggestions make interaction comfortable.
criteria.check: The tutor must invite participation using gentle suggestions or observational comments, completely avoiding direct commands or probing questions.
